list: Both flavors copy at most nLength characters of the string to szBuf, beginning with character nIndexThe number of characters copied is returnedNo null is appended. It is up to the caller to ensure szBuf is initialized to NULL or terminate the string using the returned lengthThe caller is responsible for not overflowing szBuf

The number of characters copied is returnedNo null is appended. It is up to the caller to ensure szBuf is initialized to NULL or terminate the string using the returned lengthThe caller is responsible for not overflowing szBuf
No null is appended. It is up to the caller to ensure szBuf is initialized to NULL or terminate the string using the returned lengthThe caller is responsible for not overflowing szBuf
The caller is responsible for not overflowing szBuf

content.text: Output:black

This function should be avoided where possible as it is relatively dangerous (as it is up to the caller to provide null-termination and avoid buffer overflows).Next lesson22.5std::string assignment and swappingBack to table of contentsPrevious lesson22.3std::string length and capacity
